summary: This is a very brief blog post that serves as a teaser for Joel Spolsky's Inc. magazine column called 'Start-up Static.' The post consists almost entirely of a humorous quote describing the cocky phase of startup founders, where early success goes to their heads. It links out to the full column for the complete article.
key_points: Startup founders go through a phase of extreme overconfidence after initial validation
Success in startups can breed arrogance that spills into personal relationships
The post is primarily a promotional teaser for Joel's Inc. magazine column
money_quotes: At this point, you're so dang cocky that you have too much wine at Thanksgiving dinner and pointedly remind your mother-in-law about how rude she was to dismiss your start-up idea and how, when you're making millions of dollars, there will be nothing for her — she can bloody well eat frozen government cheese.
key_insight: Startup founders pass through a phase of irrational cockiness where premature success inflates their ego far beyond what's warranted.
